rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion By Defendant Lawson Mechanical Contractors For Order Compelling Plaintiffs Answers To Deposition Questions And For Resumption Of Deposition; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Separate Statement Of Disputed Questions; Declaration Of Eliza M. Rodrigues; Lodged Certified Copies Of Deposition Transcript; (Proposed) Order
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 4, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Lawson Mechanical Contractors' motion to compel Plaintiff's answers to deposition questions and for resumption of deposition is denied. The questions Mr. Ross refused to answer seek information protected by the attorney client privilege. Other than how he recalled that he worked with Lawson at Kaiser Hospital and the Sacramento Convention Center, Mr. Ross has not disclosed a "significant part" of the contents of any communication with his attorneys. Mr. Ross answered questions relating to communications about Lawson, Kaiser Hospital, and the Sacramento Convention Center. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CALVIN SMITHSON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Calvin Smithson, Pauline Smithson, Jean Mintun, Dean Smihson, Kimberly Carter, Calvin Smithson, Jr., Douglas Smithson, Brenda Brown, Cynthia Smithson, And Gail Essary'S Motion To Compel Defedant Imo Industries, Inc. To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And To Produce Documents At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 4, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs Calvin Smithson, Pauline Smithson, Jean Mintun, Dean Smihson, Kimberly Carter, Calvin Smithson, Jr., Douglas Smithson, Brenda Brown, Cynthia Smithson, And Gail Essary's motion to compel defendant IMO Industries to produce its custodian of records and PMK for deposition and to produce documents: The parties are requested to continue to meet and confer and advise the court at the hearing what disputes remain and the parties' positions as to each remaining dispute. Hearing required unless the parties have resolved all issues covered by the motion. =(220/HEK)
